titles.title: ScrollStats: a Python tool for quantifying scroll bar morphology on meandering rivers

citation.article_title: Ground-penetrating radar and coring used to study the large-scale structure of point-bar deposits in 3 dimensions
citation.author: Bridge
citation.unstructured_citation: Bridge, J. S., Alexander, J., Collier, R. E. L., Gawthorpe, R. L., & Jarvis, J. (1995). Ground-penetrating radar and coring used to study the large-scale structure of point-bar deposits in 3 dimensions. Sedimentology, 42(6), 839–852. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-3091.1995.tb00413.x

citation.article_title: The development of meanders in natural river-channels

citation.article_title: Bank pull or bar push: What drives scroll-bar formation in meandering rivers?
citation.author: van de Lageweg
citation.unstructured_citation: van de Lageweg, W. I., van Dijk, W. M., Baar, A. W., Rutten, J., & Kleinhans, M. G. (2014). Bank pull or bar push: What drives scroll-bar formation in meandering rivers? Geology, 42(4), 319–322. https://doi.org/10.1130/G35192.1

citation.article_title: Exploring the role of trees in the evolution of meander bends: The Tagliamento River, Italy
citation.author: Zen
citation.unstructured_citation: Zen, S., Gurnell, A. M., Zolezzi, G., & Surian, N. (2017). Exploring the role of trees in the evolution of meander bends: The Tagliamento River, Italy. Water Resources Research, 53(7), 5943–5962. https://doi.org/10.1002/2017WR020561
